data.title: The Age of the Agentic CMS Is Here
data.excerpt: The CMS landscape is undergoing its biggest shift since WordPress. Here's what's happening — and why we built @webhouse/cms from scratch to meet this moment.
data.content:
## The shift

For two decades, CMS platforms optimized for one thing: making it easy for humans to type content into forms. WordPress nailed it in 2003. Contentful refined it for APIs. Sanity made it real-time.

But the paradigm is shifting. The question is no longer "how do I make it easy for a human to fill in a form?" It's "how do I make it easy for an AI agent to produce, translate, optimize, and publish content — while keeping humans in control?"

## What an agentic CMS needs

1. **A schema the AI can read.** Not a web UI. Not documentation. A machine-readable schema that tells the AI exactly what fields exist, what types they are, and what constraints apply.

2. **Native AI operations.** Generate content from a prompt. Rewrite a field. Translate to another language. Optimize SEO. These aren't plugins — they're core operations.

3. **Human override at every level.** AI Lock: field-level protection. AI generates, human approves. Human edits are sacred — AI never overwrites them.

4. **MCP protocol.** The Model Context Protocol is how AI agents discover and interact with external systems. Every @webhouse/cms site exposes two MCP servers: one public for discovery, one authenticated for production.

## What we built

40+ chat tools. AI agents for content, SEO, translation, and interactives. A media pipeline that auto-generates captions and alt-text. A build system that outputs machine-readable content (llms.txt, per-page .md files).

And the AI Lock system — the critical innovation. When a human edits a field, it locks. AI can suggest changes, but only humans can accept them. This is the trust layer that makes agentic content production viable.

## The future

The CMS of the future isn't a content editor. It's a content orchestrator. Humans set the strategy. AI executes. The CMS is the control plane.
data.author: Christian Broberg
